Annotation

Conclusion and Recommendations based on this report and previous findings known to us
The patient shows mildly elevated creatinine, suggesting possible renal impairment. Urinalysis reveals significant proteinuria, bilirubinuria, ketonuria, and abnormal urine color/appearance, with low specific gravity, indicating possible renal or hepatic dysfunction and/or dehydration. Blood glucose and HbA1c are low, raising concern for hypoglycemia. No evidence of urinary tract infection. Recommend further evaluation of renal and hepatic function, assessment for underlying metabolic or systemic disease, and clinical correlation with symptoms.
